preamble: The debuccalization of final [s] to [h] is a critical stage in the development of falling tones in several Southeast Asian language families. This laryngeal transition eventually leads to the stabilization of pitch contours as the consonant disappears entirely.
phoneticEffects: Debuccalization, Tonogenesis, Falling Tone, Laryngeal Transition

examples.note: A primary stage in Haudricourt's model where final sibilants weakened to laryngeal fricatives.
Led to the development of the Qù (去) or Departing tone.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
